overview: AI is changing the way businesses work, and organisations are looking for professionals who can build and deploy intelligent AI solutions. The Forward Deployed Architect Program is an intensive, project-based certification designed to transform software engineers and AI practitioners into enterprise-ready AI architects. This program bridges the gap between theoretical AI knowledge and practical deployment expertise, covering agentic AI systems, multi-agent architectures, enterprise RAG solutions, and autonomous SDLC practices.
courseObjective: Transform software engineers and AI practitioners into enterprise-ready AI architects capable of building and deploying agentic AI systems, multi-agent architectures, and enterprise RAG solutions at scale.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will design and deploy production-grade agentic AI systems, integrate MCP servers, build multi-agent architectures, automate SDLC workflows with AI coding assistants, and consult on AI adoption strategies with measurable ROI.
prerequisites: Basic programming knowledge in Python or JavaScript. Understanding of APIs and RESTful web services. Familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS or Azure. Experience with software development lifecycle processes.
labs: 38 hours of hands-on labs including multi-agent builds with Langgraph/AWS Strands/Google ADK, MCP Server development, Claude Code and Codex workflows, and agent evaluation with Galileo AI.
catdesc: Enterprise-focused program for transforming developers into AI architects capable of deploying agentic AI systems at scale.
whatWillYouLearn: Agentic AI system design and multi-agent architectures
LLM integration and RAG development with enterprise use cases
MCP Server development for connecting agents to third-party apps
Autonomous SDLC with AI coding assistants (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex)
AI governance, security, and responsible AI frameworks
Cloud deployment on AWS Bedrock, Azure AI Foundry, and Google platforms
AI consulting mindset and business ROI calculation
Agent evaluation with LLM-as-a-judge and Galileo AI
requirements: Basic programming knowledge in Python or JavaScript
Understanding of APIs and RESTful web services
Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS or Azure)
Experience with software development lifecycle processes

catdesc: Learn how to make cool things with AI! This course helps you create new and unique projects using simple tools
overview: LangChain Fundamentals is a hands-on introduction to building LLM-powered applications using the LangChain framework. Participants will learn to connect language models to external data sources, build conversational chatbots, and create simple chains and pipelines — all using Python. No prior LangChain experience is required.
courseObjective: Participants will understand LangChain's core components — models, prompts, chains, memory, and output parsers — and use them to build practical AI applications from scratch.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to build LangChain applications that connect LLMs to real data, implement conversational memory, parse structured outputs, and deploy simple LLM-powered tools.
prerequisites: Basic Python programming. Familiarity with LLMs (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) is helpful but not required.
labs: Hands-on labs building a document Q&A app, a conversational chatbot with memory, a structured output parser, and a simple chain pipeline using OpenAI and open-source models.
whatWillYouLearn: LangChain architecture: models, prompts, chains, memory, and output parsers
Connecting LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, HuggingFace) via LangChain model wrappers
Prompt templates: static, dynamic, and few-shot templates
Building sequential and parallel chains with LCEL (LangChain Expression Language)
Adding conversational memory: buffer, summary, and entity memory
Loading and splitting documents with LangChain document loaders and text splitters
Embedding text and storing vectors with FAISS and Chroma
Parsing structured outputs with Pydantic output parsers
requirements: Python 3.9+ installed
Basic Python programming knowledge

catdesc: Learn how to make cool things with AI! This course helps you create new and unique projects using simple tools
overview: This course is a deep dive into Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using LangChain. Participants will build production-ready RAG systems that ground LLM responses in private and enterprise data — covering advanced retrieval strategies, re-ranking, evaluation, and real-world deployment patterns.
courseObjective: Participants will design and implement advanced RAG pipelines using LangChain, covering document ingestion, vector stores, hybrid retrieval, query transformation, re-ranking, and RAG evaluation using RAGAS.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to build and evaluate production-grade RAG systems, implement hybrid and multi-query retrieval, use re-rankers to improve answer quality, and deploy RAG APIs with LangServe.
prerequisites: LangChain Fundamentals (GENAI110) or equivalent experience. Python proficiency. Basic understanding of vector embeddings.
labs: End-to-end lab building a full RAG pipeline: document ingestion → chunking → embedding → vector store → retrieval → generation → evaluation with RAGAS.
whatWillYouLearn: RAG architecture: naive RAG, advanced RAG, and modular RAG patterns
Document ingestion pipelines for PDFs, web pages, databases, and APIs
Advanced chunking: semantic chunking, parent-child chunking, and contextual chunking
Vector store options: FAISS, Chroma, Pinecone, Weaviate, and pgvector
Retrieval strategies: similarity search, MMR, and hybrid BM25 + dense retrieval
Query transformation: multi-query, HyDE, step-back, and RAG-fusion
Re-ranking with Cohere Rerank and cross-encoder models
RAG evaluation with RAGAS: faithfulness, context precision, and answer relevancy
requirements: LangChain Fundamentals or 3+ months LangChain experience
Python proficiency
API keys: OpenAI (or Anthropic) and optionally Cohere or Pinecone
targetAudience: AI Engineers building enterprise RAG systems
Data Scientists adding retrieval to LLM applications
Backend Developers building document Q&A products
LLM developers moving beyond basic chains to production RAG

content: Why RAG? Solving LLM hallucination and knowledge cutoff problems

catdesc: Learn how to make cool things with AI! This course helps you create new and unique projects using simple tools
overview: This advanced course covers building autonomous AI agents and multi-agent systems using LangChain and LangGraph. Participants will implement tool-using agents, build stateful multi-step reasoning pipelines, orchestrate agent collaboration, and deploy production-ready agentic systems.
courseObjective: Participants will design and implement LangChain agents with tool use, build stateful agent workflows with LangGraph, implement human-in-the-loop patterns, and orchestrate multi-agent collaboration for complex task solving.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to build production-grade AI agents with custom tools, implement multi-agent systems with LangGraph, add human approval steps, and monitor agentic workflows with LangSmith.
prerequisites: Strong Python skills. Completion of LangChain Fundamentals and RAG Applications (GENAI110, GENAI111) or equivalent. Familiarity with LLM APIs.

catdesc: Learn how to make cool things with AI! This course helps you create new and unique projects using simple tools
overview: This course provides a comprehensive, hands-on guide to fine-tuning large language models using Hugging Face tools. Participants will learn full fine-tuning and parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) techniques — including LoRA and QLoRA — to adapt open-source LLMs for domain-specific tasks on consumer and enterprise hardware.
courseObjective: Participants will master the Hugging Face Trainer API, SFTTrainer, and PEFT library to fine-tune models like Llama, Mistral, and Phi for classification, instruction following, and domain adaptation tasks.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to prepare datasets for fine-tuning, apply LoRA and QLoRA adapters, train models with Hugging Face Trainer and SFTTrainer, evaluate fine-tuned models, and push them to the Hugging Face Hub.
prerequisites: Hugging Face fundamentals (GENAI103) or equivalent. Python proficiency. Basic understanding of neural networks and transformers.
labs: Hands-on labs fine-tuning Llama/Mistral for instruction following, applying LoRA adapters with PEFT, quantising with bitsandbytes (QLoRA), and evaluating with ROUGE and BERTScore.
whatWillYouLearn: When to fine-tune vs prompt engineer vs use RAG
Preparing and formatting instruction-tuning datasets with Hugging Face Datasets
Full fine-tuning with the Hugging Face Trainer API
Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT): LoRA and QLoRA techniques
Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with TRL's SFTTrainer
4-bit quantisation with bitsandbytes for training on consumer GPUs
Chat template formatting for instruction-tuned models
Evaluating fine-tuned models with ROUGE, BERTScore, and LLM-as-judge
Pushing fine-tuned models and adapters to Hugging Face Hub
requirements: Hugging Face Transformers experience
Python proficiency
GPU access (Google Colab Pro or equivalent recommended)
targetAudience: ML Engineers adapting LLMs for specific domains
Data Scientists building custom NLP models
AI Researchers experimenting with open-source LLMs

Students will learn how to leverage Azure Databricks for model training, hyperparameter tuning, and deploying machine learning models, with a focus on practical application and integration with MLflow and AutoML.
courseObjective: To provide students with the skills necessary to use Azure Databricks for end-to-end machine learning workflows, including model training, hyperparameter tuning, and deploying models.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of machine learning concepts
Familiarity with Python programming
Understanding of data science processes
labs: Labs include practical exercises for preparing data, training models, using MLflow for tracking experiments, tuning hyperparameters, and deploying models. Step-by-step instructions are provided within the lab environment, which may be updated as Microsoft releases new UI changes.
whatWillYouLearn: Exploring Azure Databricks as a data lake analytics platform
Training and evaluating machine learning models
Using MLflow for experiment tracking and model deployment
Tuning hyperparameters with Hyperopt
Leveraging AutoML for automated machine learning tasks
Training and distributing deep learning models
requirements: Access to Azure Databricks
Basic understanding of Python and machine learning

content: Introduction to Azure Databricks

overview: This course provides comprehensive training on using Power BI for data analysis, visualization, and reporting. It covers various aspects from data preparation and modeling to building interactive reports and dashboards, with a focus on best practices for both business and technical requirements.

courseTitle: AZ-204: Developing Solutions for Microsoft Azure
overview: This course is designed for Azure Developers and covers the key aspects of developing cloud solutions using Microsoft Azure. It includes managing Azure subscriptions, securing identities, configuring networking, implementing storage solutions, and monitoring applications. Students will learn to deploy and manage applications, integrate with Azure services, and enhance solution performance and security.

prerequisites: Experience in software development
Basic understanding of cloud concepts

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
courseTitle: CRISC (Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control)
overview: The CRISC (Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control) course is designed for IT and business professionals who identify and manage risks through the development, implementation, and maintenance of Information Systems Controls. Learners gain expertise in the governance and application of risk management strategies to enhance the value of their organization's IT and business systems.
courseObjective: Participants will develop a comprehensive understanding of IT risk management, including risk identification, assessment, response, and reporting, along with the design and implementation of IS controls aligned to business objectives.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to identify and evaluate IT risks, implement risk response strategies, design effective IS controls, and apply governance frameworks — preparing them for the ISACA CRISC certification exam.
prerequisites: 2–3 years of experience in IT risk management or IS control is recommended. Familiarity with IT governance frameworks (COBIT, ISO 31000) is beneficial.

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
courseTitle: Certified in the Governance of Enterprise IT (CGEIT)
overview: The Certified in the Governance of Enterprise IT (CGEIT) course, offered by ISACA, is a comprehensive program that equips learners with the necessary knowledge and skills to govern enterprise IT effectively. It promotes the use of best practices and alignment with business goals, ensuring IT resources are managed efficiently and responsibly. CGEIT focuses on four key domains, with Domain 1: Governance of Enterprise IT accounting for 40% of the curriculum, covering governance frameworks, strategy development, legal compliance, stakeholder engagement, and information asset management.
courseObjective: Participants will develop advanced capabilities in enterprise IT governance, learning to align IT strategy with business objectives, optimize IT resources, realize IT-enabled benefits, and manage risk effectively across the organization.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to design and implement IT governance frameworks, manage IT resources and investments, drive benefits realization, and apply risk optimization strategies — preparing them for the ISACA CGEIT certification exam.
prerequisites: 5+ years of experience in IT governance or a related field is recommended. Familiarity with COBIT or similar IT governance frameworks is beneficial.
labs: Case studies and scenario-based exercises are included throughout each domain.

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
overview: COBIT 2019 (Control Objectives for Information and Related Technologies) is ISACA's globally recognized framework for IT governance and management. This 3-day course provides a comprehensive understanding of the COBIT 2019 framework, its core principles, governance and management objectives, and how to design and implement a tailored governance system for any enterprise. Participants learn to align IT strategy with business goals, optimize risk, and demonstrate value from IT investments.
courseObjective: Equip IT governance professionals with the knowledge to design, implement, and continually improve an enterprise IT governance system using the COBIT 2019 framework, governance design factors, and performance management practices.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to apply the COBIT 2019 core model, design a governance system using design factors, map governance and management objectives to enterprise needs, and measure IT performance — preparing them for the ISACA COBIT Foundation and Design & Implementation certifications.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of IT governance concepts is recommended. Experience in IT management, risk, or audit is advantageous. No prior COBIT knowledge required for Foundation level.
labs: Hands-on workshops applying governance design factors, mapping objectives, and building a sample governance system for a case-study enterprise.

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
overview: ISO 31000:2018 is the international standard for risk management, providing universal principles, a flexible framework, and a structured process applicable to any organization regardless of size, sector, or industry. This 2-day course covers the complete ISO 31000 standard — from foundational principles and leadership commitment to risk identification, assessment, treatment, monitoring, and continual improvement. Participants gain practical skills to embed risk management into organizational decision-making and governance.
courseObjective: Provide participants with a thorough understanding of the ISO 31000:2018 standard, enabling them to design, implement, and continually improve an enterprise risk management framework aligned with international best practices.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to apply ISO 31000 principles, establish a risk management framework, execute the risk management process (including identification, analysis, evaluation, and treatment), and integrate risk management into organizational governance.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of organizational management or governance is recommended. No prior risk management certification required.
labs: Practical workshops on risk identification, risk register development, risk assessment techniques, and risk treatment planning using real-world organizational scenarios.

courseObjective: Participants will gain expertise in designing and implementing a security strategy on Google Cloud, including identity and access management, data encryption, network perimeter controls, threat detection, and compliance automation.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to secure GCP workloads end-to-end, implement zero-trust principles, respond to security findings in Security Command Center, and pass the Google Professional Cloud Security Engineer certification exam.
prerequisites: 2+ years of experience in cloud security or information security
Familiarity with Google Cloud fundamentals (ACE level recommended)
Understanding of IAM concepts, encryption fundamentals, and networking security
A Google Cloud account (free tier available with $300 USD credits)
labs: Hands-on labs cover IAM policy bindings, Cloud KMS key management, Secret Manager configuration, VPC Service Controls setup, Cloud Armor WAF rules, Security Command Center findings, Binary Authorization for GKE, and Cloud DLP sensitive data inspection.
whatWillYouLearn: Configure and manage Google Cloud IAM including custom roles and workload identity
Implement data protection using Cloud KMS, Secret Manager, and CMEK
Secure network perimeters with VPC Service Controls, Cloud Armor, and firewall policies
Detect and respond to threats using Security Command Center and Chronicle
Implement compliance controls for HIPAA, PCI-DSS, GDPR, and SOC 2
Manage supply chain security with Binary Authorization and Artifact Registry
Automate security operations and enforce governance using organization policies
requirements: Access to a Google Cloud account
Understanding of cloud security concepts and IAM principles
targetAudience: Information Security Professionals moving to GCP
DevSecOps Engineers and Platform Security leads
Compliance and Risk Officers managing cloud environments

catdesc: Master Google Cloud Platform to design and build scalable, secure, and reliable cloud solutions. These courses prepare you for Google Cloud certifications with real-world architecture scenarios and hands-on lab practice.

Covering tenant management, identity synchronisation, security, compliance, and Teams administration, this course aligns with the Microsoft 365 Certified: Administrator Expert certification.
courseObjective: Participants will gain skills to manage Microsoft 365 tenants, configure hybrid identity, implement security and compliance policies, manage Microsoft Teams, and govern Microsoft 365 services at enterprise scale.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to manage Microsoft 365 user provisioning, configure Entra ID hybrid identity, implement Defender for Microsoft 365, manage compliance policies with Purview, and pass the MS-102 exam.

catdesc: Master Microsoft Power Platform to build low-code solutions, automate business processes, and analyse data across your organisation. These courses cover Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, and Power Virtual Agents for developers and functional consultants.

Participants will work with Dataverse, model-driven and canvas apps, Power Automate flows, Power Virtual Agents chatbots, and Power BI dashboards — preparing them for the Microsoft Certified: Power Platform Functional Consultant Associate exam.

catdesc: Master Atlassian tools including Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, and Jira Service Management to streamline project management, IT service delivery, and DevOps workflows for teams of all sizes.
overview: This course introduces participants to Jira Software — the world's most popular project tracking tool. Participants will learn to create and manage projects, configure boards, track issues, and run Agile ceremonies using Jira's Scrum and Kanban frameworks. Aligned with the ACP-100 certification objectives.
courseObjective: Participants will gain hands-on proficiency in navigating Jira Software, creating and configuring projects, managing backlogs, running sprints, and using Jira's reporting tools to track team progress.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to independently manage Jira projects, configure Scrum and Kanban boards, create epics, stories and sub-tasks, use filters and dashboards, and generate sprint and velocity reports.
prerequisites: No prior Jira experience required. Basic familiarity with software development or project management concepts is beneficial.
labs: Hands-on labs covering project setup, board configuration, issue creation and management, sprint planning, and dashboard creation in a live Jira Cloud environment.

Use JQL (Jira Query Language) for advanced searching

requirements: Access to a Jira Cloud account (free trial available)
Basic understanding of Agile or project management concepts

catdesc: Master modern DevOps practices including containers, orchestration, CI/CD, and infrastructure as code. Build, deploy, and scale applications with industry-standard tools used by leading engineering teams.

catdesc: Master modern DevOps practices including containers, orchestration, CI/CD, and infrastructure as code. Build, deploy, and scale applications with industry-standard tools used by leading engineering teams.

catdesc: Master Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) and learn how to integrate AI services — from REST API calls to Generative AI workflows — into enterprise integration solutions.
overview: This comprehensive course covers Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) from the ground up and extends into cutting-edge AI integration — including calling AI and Generative AI services, automating business workflows with AI, and building event-driven AI-powered integrations. Participants gain hands-on experience building real-world enterprise integration solutions enriched with AI capabilities.
courseObjective: Equip integration professionals and enterprise architects with the skills to design, build, and manage OIC integrations — including AI service integrations, GenAI workflow automation, and API management — so they can deliver intelligent, automated enterprise solutions.
courseOutcomes: Participants will be able to create and manage integrations on Oracle Integration Cloud, call AI and Generative AI APIs from OIC workflows, build event-driven and scheduled automations, implement fault handling and performance optimisation, manage APIs with OIC API Management, and apply security and governance best practices.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of REST APIs and web services. Familiarity with enterprise application concepts is helpful. No prior OIC experience required.

catdesc: Equip Product Owners and Product Managers with Gen AI skills to accelerate ideation, backlog refinement, roadmap planning, and data-driven product decisions.
overview: This course empowers Product Owners and Product Managers to embed Generative AI into every stage of the product lifecycle — from competitive research and persona creation to backlog refinement, prototype generation, and roadmap optimisation. Using tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot, participants gain hands-on experience building AI-enhanced product workflows that save time and sharpen decision-making.
courseObjective: Give product professionals practical AI skills to accelerate discovery, prioritisation, and delivery — using leading Gen AI tools without requiring any technical background.
courseOutcomes: Participants will be able to conduct AI-powered competitive and market research, generate detailed customer personas and user stories with AI, prioritise backlogs and plan roadmaps using AI-driven insights, create low-fidelity prototypes and product concepts with AI tools, and design effective prompts to extract actionable product intelligence.
prerequisites: No prior AI or programming experience required. Familiarity with basic product management concepts (backlog, sprint, roadmap) is sufficient.
labs: Hands-on labs include: generating a competitive landscape report using AI, building customer personas from interview transcripts, writing and refining user stories with acceptance criteria using AI, producing a prioritised backlog with AI scoring, and creating a visual product roadmap outline with AI assistance.

overview: This comprehensive 24-hour intensive course provides participants with in-depth knowledge of industrial cybersecurity aligned with SANS ICS410 principles. Participants will explore the industrial control system security landscape, SCADA systems vulnerabilities, threat modeling, defense mechanisms, governance frameworks, and incident response strategies specific to critical infrastructure protection.

catdesc: Master the art of managing organizational change with ACMP-aligned certification training
overview: This comprehensive 4-day course prepares participants for the CCMP™ certification, covering the ACMP® Standard Second Edition (2025). Participants will develop expertise in change impact assessment, strategic planning, plan development and execution, and organizational readiness. The course includes exam preparation and a mock exam to ensure readiness for the certification.

Participants will develop strategies to minimize resistance, maximize adoption, and ensure sustainable change outcomes.

catdesc: Build cross-platform mobile apps for iOS and Android using JavaScript and React Native.
overview: This intensive 5-day course teaches developers how to build production-quality cross-platform mobile applications for iOS and Android using React Native and Expo. Participants move from environment setup through to app store deployment, covering navigation, state management, REST API integration, native device features, UI/UX design, automated testing, and performance optimisation. Each module includes hands-on labs using real device emulators and physical testing.
courseObjective: Equip participants with the skills to architect, build, test, and deploy React Native applications. Learners will gain practical experience with the full mobile development lifecycle — from scaffolding a new project with Expo to publishing to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store.
prerequisites: Solid working knowledge of JavaScript (ES6+) and React fundamentals. Basic understanding of HTML and CSS. No prior mobile development experience required.
labs: Hands-on labs using Expo CLI, Android Studio emulator, Xcode Simulator, React Navigation, Redux Toolkit, Axios, React Native Testing Library, and EAS Build.

Integrate RESTful APIs and handle async data with Axios, Fetch, and React Query
Access native device features including camera, GPS, push notifications, and local storage
Write and run unit, integration, and end-to-end tests using Jest and Detox
Build, sign, and deploy apps to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store using EAS Build
requirements: JavaScript (ES6+): arrow functions, destructuring, async/await, modules
React fundamentals: components, props, state, hooks (useState, useEffect)
A laptop with Node.js 18+ installed (Windows, macOS, or Linux)
Android Studio or Xcode installed (or willingness to install before Day 1)

catdesc: Master MuleSoft Anypoint Platform to build, deploy, and manage integrations and APIs.
courseTitle: MuleSoft: Integration Architecture with Anypoint Platform
overview: This advanced 4-day course is designed for integration architects and senior developers who need to design and govern enterprise integration solutions on the MuleSoft Anypoint Platform. Topics include API-led connectivity strategy, deployment model selection, application network design, security architecture, and governance. The course is the primary preparation path for the MuleSoft Certified Integration Architect (MCIA) Level 1 certification.
courseObjective: Enable participants to design scalable, secure, and governed integration architectures using the Anypoint Platform. Learners will evaluate deployment models, design application networks, define governance frameworks, and make technology selection decisions aligned with business requirements. Prepares for MCIA Level 1 certification.
prerequisites: Solid hands-on MuleSoft development experience (minimum 1 year). Completion of MS101, MS102, and MS104 recommended. Understanding of enterprise integration patterns (EIP) and cloud architecture concepts.
labs: Architecture design workshops, case study analysis, Anypoint Platform configuration exercises covering Runtime Fabric, API Manager governance, and multi-environment topology design.

catdesc: Build world-class sales skills with globally recognized certification training. These courses cover consultative selling, negotiation, account management, and sales leadership to help professionals win more business and grow revenue.
overview: The CPSP™ (Certified Professional Sales Person) is one of the most recognized sales certifications, developed by the National Association of Sales Professionals (NASP). This 3-day course equips sales professionals with a proven, psychology-driven sales methodology — moving beyond traditional selling techniques to build genuine relationships, understand buyer behaviour, and close deals consistently. The curriculum covers the complete sales cycle from prospecting and discovery to objection handling, negotiation, and post-sale relationship management.
courseObjective: Develop participants into confident, high-performing sales professionals who can apply a consultative, buyer-centric sales approach to shorten sales cycles, increase win rates, and build lasting client relationships.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to execute a full consultative sales process, handle objections with confidence, apply influence and persuasion principles ethically, manage a sales pipeline effectively, and prepare for the NASP CPSP™ certification.
prerequisites: No formal prerequisites. Basic experience in a sales, business development, or customer-facing role is recommended.
labs: Role-play exercises, live sales call simulations, objection handling workshops, and pipeline management case studies.

Building a personal brand and professional credibility in sales

catdesc: Master the science and practice of learning design, training delivery, and L&D strategy. These courses equip HR and L&D professionals with globally recognized frameworks and practical tools to build high-impact learning cultures.
courseTitle: CLDP: Certified Learning and Development Professional
overview: The CLDP (Certified Learning and Development Professional) course is designed for HR professionals, trainers, and L&D practitioners who want to design, deliver, and evaluate high-impact learning experiences. This 3-day certification covers the full L&D lifecycle — from training needs analysis (TNA) and instructional design to facilitation skills, digital learning tools, and L&D strategy — aligned with globally recognized frameworks including ATD, CIPD, and ISO 29993.
courseObjective: Equip L&D professionals with the knowledge, frameworks, and practical skills to design effective learning programs, facilitate engaging training sessions, measure learning impact, and contribute to organizational capability building.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to conduct training needs analyses, design structured learning programs using ADDIE and other ISD models, facilitate instructor-led and virtual training effectively, apply measurement frameworks (Kirkpatrick, Phillips ROI), and develop an L&D strategy aligned with business goals.
prerequisites: Basic experience in HR, training, or people management is recommended. No prior L&D certification required.

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
courseTitle: CISSP – Certified Information Systems Security Professional
overview: The CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional) course is the gold standard for cybersecurity professionals seeking to validate their expertise across all eight domains of information security. Developed by (ISC)², this globally recognized certification equips security leaders with the knowledge and skills to effectively design, implement, and manage a best-in-class cybersecurity program. Participants will master a broad range of security disciplines — from risk management and asset security to software development security and cryptography.
courseObjective: Participants will gain deep expertise across all eight CISSP Common Body of Knowledge (CBK) domains, develop the ability to think like a security manager rather than a practitioner, and prepare thoroughly for the (ISC)² CISSP certification exam.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to design and oversee enterprise security programs, apply risk management principles across all security domains, implement access controls and identity management strategies, ensure secure software development lifecycles, and demonstrate the comprehensive knowledge required for the CISSP certification.
prerequisites: A minimum of 5 years of cumulative, paid work experience in at least two of the eight CISSP CBK domains is required for full certification. Professionals with less experience may attend the course and earn the Associate of (ISC)² designation upon passing the exam.
labs: Domain-specific scenario exercises, case studies, and practice questions aligned to the (ISC)² CISSP CAT exam format are included throughout the course.

Communication and network security — from OSI model to secure design
Identity and access management (IAM) frameworks and technologies
Security assessment and testing strategies, including audits and vulnerability analysis
Security operations — incident response, DR, BCP, and forensic investigation
Software development security — SDLC, DevSecOps, and API security

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
overview: The CISM (Certified Information Security Manager) course is designed for professionals who manage, design, oversee, and assess an enterprise's information security. Recognized globally by ISACA, the CISM certification validates the expertise needed to develop and manage a comprehensive information security program aligned to business goals. This course covers all four CISM domains and prepares candidates for the ISACA CISM examination.
courseObjective: Participants will develop the management-level knowledge needed to design and govern information security programs, manage information risk aligned to business objectives, and oversee incident response and recovery — preparing them for the ISACA CISM certification.

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
overview: The CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor) course is the globally recognized standard for IS audit, control, and security professionals. Offered by ISACA, the CISA certification demonstrates expertise in auditing, monitoring, controlling, and assessing IT and business systems. This course prepares participants to audit information systems effectively, assess enterprise IT governance, and evaluate information asset protection — covering all five CISA domains.
courseObjective: Participants will gain the knowledge and skills to perform effective IS audits, evaluate IT governance and risk management practices, assess systems acquisition and development controls, review IT operations and resilience, and analyze information asset protection — preparing them for the ISACA CISA certification exam.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to plan and conduct IS audits in accordance with ISACA standards, evaluate IT governance and management frameworks, assess controls throughout the SDLC, review business continuity and disaster recovery plans, and determine the adequacy of information security controls.
prerequisites: A minimum of 5 years of professional work experience in IS auditing, control, or security is required for full CISA certification. Substitutions and waivers of up to 3 years are available. Basic understanding of audit concepts is recommended.

catdesc: Build skills to identify, manage, and control IT risks. These courses prepare you for industry-recognized certifications and teach you how to protect organizations through governance, risk assessment, and security frameworks.
overview: The CSX-P (Cybersecurity Practitioner) certification by ISACA is a performance-based credential that validates hands-on cybersecurity skills aligned to the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF). Unlike knowledge-only exams, CSX-P tests real-world ability to identify threats, protect systems, detect attacks, respond to incidents, and recover operations. This course prepares participants to demonstrate practical cybersecurity competence across all five NIST CSF functions.
courseObjective: Participants will develop hands-on proficiency across the five NIST CSF functions — Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, and Recover — and build the practical skills needed to pass the ISACA CSX-P performance-based certification examination.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to conduct cybersecurity risk identification and asset management, implement protective controls, detect and analyze security events, execute incident response procedures, and lead recovery operations — demonstrating real-world cybersecurity capability.
prerequisites: 2+ years of hands-on cybersecurity experience is recommended. Familiarity with networking fundamentals, operating systems, and basic security tools. CompTIA Security+ or equivalent knowledge is beneficial.
labs: Hands-on lab exercises covering vulnerability scanning, log analysis, intrusion detection, incident response simulations, and recovery procedures using industry-standard tools.

catdesc: Automate repetitive tasks and connect your apps without code. These courses teach you to build powerful workflows using n8n — from simple integrations to AI-powered enterprise automation pipelines.
courseTitle: n8n Fundamentals: Workflow Automation for Everyone
overview: n8n Fundamentals: Workflow Automation for Everyone is a hands-on introduction to n8n — the open-source, self-hostable workflow automation platform. n8n lets you connect apps, APIs, and databases visually, without writing code, making it the ideal tool for ops teams, business analysts, and developers who want to automate repetitive processes fast. This course covers n8n's core architecture, building multi-step workflows, connecting popular apps, and deploying automations to production — both on n8n Cloud and self-hosted environments.
courseObjective: Participants will learn to navigate the n8n interface, build automated workflows using trigger and action nodes, connect to popular apps and APIs, handle data transformations, manage errors, and schedule and deploy workflows reliably.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to design and deploy n8n workflows that automate real business processes, connect apps using pre-built integrations, transform data with expressions, handle errors gracefully, and run automations on schedules or webhooks.
prerequisites: No programming experience required. Basic familiarity with web apps (email, spreadsheets, CRMs) is sufficient. A free n8n Cloud account or local installation will be used.
labs: Hands-on labs building a lead capture workflow, a multi-app notification pipeline, a scheduled data sync, and a webhook-triggered automation — using real app integrations throughout.
whatWillYouLearn: n8n architecture: workflows, nodes, triggers, connections, and executions
Navigating the n8n editor: canvas, node panel, and execution history
Trigger nodes: Webhook, Schedule, and app-specific event triggers
Core action nodes: HTTP Request, Set, IF, Switch, Merge, and Loop
Connecting popular apps: Gmail, Slack, Google Sheets, Notion, Airtable, and more
Data transformation with n8n expressions and the JMESPath syntax
Error handling: try/catch patterns, error workflows, and notifications
Deploying workflows: n8n Cloud, Docker self-hosting, and activation management
requirements: A computer with internet access
Free n8n Cloud account or Docker installed for self-hosted setup
No programming knowledge required
targetAudience: Business Analysts automating manual data workflows
IT Admins connecting internal systems and tools
Marketing and Sales teams automating CRM and outreach workflows
Startup Founders building lean automated business processes
Non-developers wanting to automate without writing code
Developers evaluating n8n as an alternative to Zapier or Make

catdesc: Automate repetitive tasks and connect your apps without code. These courses teach you to build powerful workflows using n8n — from simple integrations to AI-powered enterprise automation pipelines.
courseTitle: n8n for AI Workflows: Agents, LLMs, and Automation
overview: n8n for AI Workflows: Agents, LLMs, and Automation teaches you to harness n8n's powerful AI nodes to build intelligent automation workflows powered by large language models. n8n has first-class support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and open-source models via Ollama — and includes built-in AI Agent, Chain, and Memory nodes for building autonomous AI pipelines visually. This course is designed for AI engineers and developers who want to combine the power of LLMs with n8n's 400+ app integrations to build real-world AI automation products — without managing infrastructure.
courseObjective: Participants will learn to use n8n's AI nodes to connect LLMs to workflows, build AI agent loops with tool use and memory, create RAG pipelines with vector store integration, and deploy production AI automation workflows that combine LLM reasoning with real app actions.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to build n8n AI agent workflows that call LLMs, use tools, retain memory, and take actions across connected apps — creating end-to-end AI automation pipelines from prompt to production.
prerequisites: n8n Fundamentals (N8N101) or equivalent experience. Basic understanding of LLMs (ChatGPT or similar) is helpful. No Python required.
labs: Hands-on labs building an AI email triage agent, a RAG-powered document Q&A workflow, an AI content generation pipeline, and an autonomous multi-tool AI agent connected to real apps.
whatWillYouLearn: n8n AI nodes: AI Agent, Basic LLM Chain, Summarization Chain, and Question & Answer Chain
Connecting LLMs: OpenAI (GPT-4o), Anthropic (Claude), Google Gemini, and Ollama
Building AI Agent nodes with tool use: web search, code execution, and custom tools
Memory nodes: Window Buffer Memory and Postgres Chat Memory for persistent context
Vector store integration: Pinecone, Qdrant, and Supabase for RAG workflows
Document loaders and text splitters for ingesting and chunking content
AI-powered automation patterns: email triage, content generation, and data extraction
Combining AI reasoning with n8n app actions for end-to-end automation
requirements: n8n Cloud account or self-hosted n8n (v1.0+)
OpenAI API key or compatible LLM provider account
Completion of N8N101 or equivalent n8n experience

catdesc: Automate repetitive tasks and connect your apps without code. These courses teach you to build powerful workflows using n8n — from simple integrations to AI-powered enterprise automation pipelines.
courseTitle: Advanced n8n: Custom Nodes, APIs, and Enterprise Deployment
overview: Advanced n8n: Custom Nodes, APIs, and Enterprise Deployment takes experienced n8n users to the next level — building custom nodes in TypeScript, integrating with internal APIs and enterprise systems, applying advanced data transformation patterns, hardening n8n for production with Docker and Kubernetes, and designing scalable multi-instance enterprise architectures. This course is designed for developers, DevOps engineers, and enterprise architects who need to extend n8n beyond its built-in capabilities and run it reliably at scale.
courseObjective: Participants will learn to develop and publish custom n8n nodes using TypeScript, build secure enterprise integrations via webhooks and OAuth2, apply advanced transformation patterns, self-host n8n in production environments, and architect multi-instance enterprise deployments with high availability.
courseOutcomes: Upon completion, participants will be able to build and publish custom n8n nodes, integrate n8n with any internal or enterprise API securely, self-host n8n using Docker/Kubernetes with PostgreSQL and Redis, and design fault-tolerant enterprise n8n architectures.
prerequisites: n8n Fundamentals (N8N101) or solid n8n experience. TypeScript/JavaScript familiarity required for custom node development modules. Basic Docker knowledge helpful for deployment modules.
labs: Hands-on labs building a custom n8n node published to a local registry, a secure OAuth2 API integration, an advanced data pipeline with JMESPath, and a production Docker Compose n8n deployment with PostgreSQL and Redis.
whatWillYouLearn: Custom node development: node structure, types, credentials, and TypeScript patterns
Testing and publishing custom nodes to npm and private registries
Advanced data transformation: JMESPath, JavaScript Code node, and Function Item
OAuth2 credential flows and API authentication patterns in n8n
Webhook hardening: HMAC signatures, IP filtering, and rate limiting
n8n self-hosting: Docker Compose with PostgreSQL, Redis, and S3 binary data
Kubernetes deployment with n8n Helm charts and horizontal scaling
Enterprise architecture: queue mode, worker nodes, and multi-instance setups
requirements: Solid n8n experience (N8N101 or equivalent)
TypeScript/JavaScript knowledge for custom node development
Basic Docker and command-line familiarity
Node.js installed locally

DevOps Engineers deploying and maintaining n8n in production

overview: The AI for CXOs Workshop is a 2-day intensive program designed to equip CEOs, CFOs, CTOs, CIOs, CDOs, and COOs with the strategic AI knowledge needed to drive innovation, competitive advantage, and measurable business outcomes. This leadership workshop gives senior executives a structured understanding of artificial intelligence — from GenAI and LLMs to real-world adoption strategies — so they can make confident, informed AI decisions at the highest level.

overview: The AI-Native Development Using BDD course is an advanced hands-on training program designed for professionals who want to integrate AI-assisted development practices into modern Agile software engineering workflows. This training introduces spec-driven development using BDD as the primary mechanism to guide AI systems during software creation, demonstrating how Agile teams use executable specifications, prompts, and AI-native workflows to accelerate software delivery.
courseObjective: Equip Agile software teams with practical skills to integrate BDD practices with AI-assisted programming, enabling spec-driven development that guides AI systems to generate maintainable, testable, and well-structured software.
courseOutcomes: Participants will earn the BDD for Agile Teams microcredential and be able to write executable specifications in Gherkin, facilitate BDD discovery workshops, integrate BDD into Scrum workflows, and apply AI-native engineering practices to accelerate software delivery.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of software development or Agile principles. Familiarity with Scrum or software delivery workflows is recommended.
